title: synaphea macrophylla
intro: Synaphea macrophylla, also known as the Western synaphea or large leaved synaphea, is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia. It is a shrub that has been utilized for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and fever, and its extracts have also been found to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. The leaves and stems of Synaphea macrophylla can be prepared as a tea or infused in oil to utilize its medicinal properties, and it is also used in traditional remedies as a poultice or salve.
